briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Initialize the cancellable handler: create input/output queues and spawn a background child process for parsing.

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Request request, dict parameters, str file_path, str tmpdir)
memberdef.name: handle

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Handle a parsing request with client disconnect support.

detaileddescription.para: Sends task to child process via queue, monitors connection, terminates child on disconnect. \par \b Uses\/ \: cancel_on_disconnect, loop.run_in_executor \par \b I/O\/ \: (Request, dict, str, str) -> Optional[ParsedDocument] \par \b Complexity\/ \: 9
Handle request in a separate process. Checks for client disconnection and terminate the child process if client disconnected.

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Background child process entry point: waits for tasks on input_queue, parses documents via DedocManager, and returns results via output_queue.

detaileddescription.para: Uses signal isolation to protect the master process. \par \b Uses\/ \: DedocManager.parse, pickle.loads, pickle.dumps \par \b I/O\/ \: (Queue, Queue) -> None (runs indefinitely) \par \b Complexity\/ \: 9
Function for file parsing in a separate (child) process. It's a background process, i.e. it is waiting for a task in the input queue. The result of parsing is returned in the output queue.
Operations with signal are used for saving master process while killing child process. See the issue for more details: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues/1487

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Handler for file parsing with support for client disconnection.

detaileddescription.para: Spawns a child process for CPU-bound parsing and terminates it on disconnect, keeping the API available for new connections.

Algorithm:
Master process monitors client connection (via cancel_on_disconnect)
Child process waits on input_queue for tasks
Master sends task via pickle to input_queue
Child parses using DedocManager
Result is sent back via output_queue (pickle)
On disconnect: child process terminated, new process spawned on next request

Class for file parsing by DedocManager with support for client disconnection. If client disconnects during file parsing, the process of parsing is fully terminated and API is available to receive new connections.

Handler uses the following algorithm:
Master process is used for checking current connection (client disconnect)
Child process is working on the background and waiting for the input file in the input_queue
Master process calls the child process for parsing and transfers data through the input_queue
Child process is parsing file using DedocManager
The result of parsing is transferred to the master process through the output_queue
If client disconnects, the child process is terminated. The new child process with queues will start with the new request
